compile_error: MACA mxcc failed with four type errors in the all64 gate/up GEMMs: each generated FP16 accumulator operand has type float16x4, but the MACA GEMM intrinsic parameter requires float4. Representative diagnostic: cannot initialize a parameter of type vector of 4 float values with an lvalue of type float16x4.

resource_notes.change: Only _moe_forward_kernel_all64 was changed. The former accum_dtype was split into gate_accum_dtype=float16 and down_accum_dtype=float32; gate_local_0, gate_local_1, up_local_0, and up_local_1 use gate_accum_dtype, while out_local_0 and out_local_1 explicitly use down_accum_dtype. Tile, thread, pack, loop, and swizzle settings are unchanged.
resource_notes.small_shape: The requested hidden=512, intermediate=512, experts=4, valid=568, seed=81394 case reached MACA device compilation but did not produce a runnable kernel.

correctness_metrics.reason: No output was produced because device compilation failed.

resource_notes.compiler_details: Generated locals were half_t arrays and GEMM operands were loaded as float16x4. The existing MACA MMA macro accepts float4 accumulator vectors, producing four hard errors corresponding to the four gate/up accumulators. Compiler warnings about uninitialized internal cc0-cc3 variables and ignored minimum block counts preceded the hard type errors but were not the stopping cause.
resource_notes.early_stop: Stopped immediately after small-shape compilation failure. No formal points or random-groups cases were run. Changing the GEMM intrinsic, policy, or accumulator interface would exceed this experiment's single variable.
